title: Callipteris ceratolepis
taxonomy: Callipteris ceratolepis is a genus of extinct ferns within the family Matoniaceae. The family Matoniaceae is part of the class Polypodiopsida, which includes all types of ferns. The order of this family is Matoniales, a distinct order within the class. The class Polypodiopsida is further grouped into the division Pteridophyta, which comprises all ferns and their allies. This classification indicates the evolutionary relationships and characteristics of the extinct fern Callipteris ceratolepis.
uses: Callipteris ceratolepis is known for its traditional medicinal uses in certain regions. The plant's extracts have been used to treat various health issues, including fever and inflammation. In traditional medicine, it is believed to have antipyretic and anti-inflammatory properties. However, further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and potential side effects. Its medicinal uses may vary depending on the specific application and cultural context.
intro: There is not enough information available about the plant Callipteris ceratolepis.
